original_hypothesis.title: GaugeFactoryCL.createEternalFarming Requires Pre-funded Factory But No Funding Mechanism Exists
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In GaugeFactoryCL.createGauge(), the internal function createEternalFarming() approves 1e10 (10 billion in base units) of reward tokens from the factory to AlgebraEternalFarming and calls addRewards. However, the factory contract has no mechanism to receive these reward tokens. If the factory's reward token balance is less than 1e10, the safeApprove and addRewards calls will fail, reverting the entire gauge creation transaction for CL pools.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The factory needs to hold reward tokens (BLACK) to seed the initial farming incentive. No transfer-in function exists in GaugeFactoryCL. The deployment scripts don't show any pre-funding step. This blocks all CL gauge creation until manually resolved by sending tokens directly to the factory.
deployment_assumptions: GaugeFactoryCL must be pre-funded with at least 1e10 base units of reward token (BLACK)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: GaugeFactoryCL.sol lines 42-57: approves and calls addRewards with reward=1e10 but no receive mechanism. No deployment script funds the factory.

proof_of_signal.pattern: IERC20(_rewardToken).safeApprove(_algebraEternalFarming, reward) where reward=1e10; factory has no token receipt mechanism

preconditions: GaugeFactoryCL reward token balance < 1e10 base units
Any attempt to create a CL gauge

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: uint128 reward = 1e10; IERC20(_rewardToken).safeApprove(_algebraEternalFarming, reward); but factory has no tokens
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Not an attack - operational defect blocking CL gauge creation
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: N/A - DoS on CL gauge creation

economic_questions: Has the factory been manually funded with reward tokens?
How many CL gauges need to be created (each consuming 1e10)?
validation_requirements: Check factory balance of reward token
Verify no alternative funding path exists
Confirm safeApprove reverts on insufficient balance

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Protocol operators and CL liquidity providers
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Cannot create CL gauges for new pools

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Factory reward token balance, 1e10 in BLACK terms
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm addRewards transfers tokens from factory

validation_result.mechanism: unfunded factory blocks CL gauge creation
validation_result.impact_summary: GaugeFactoryCL.createEternalFarming() requires the factory to hold reward tokens (1e10 base units per gauge) but the contract has no built-in funding mechanism and no deployment script funds it, causing all CL gauge creation to revert.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The createEternalFarming() internal function in GaugeFactoryCL.sol approves 1e10 base units of the reward token to AlgebraEternalFarming and then calls createEternalFarming() on that contract with IncentiveParams containing reward=1e10. The external AlgebraEternalFarming contract will attempt to transferFrom these tokens from GaugeFactoryCL. If the factory does not hold at least 1e10 base units, this transferFrom will revert, causing the entire gauge creation transaction to fail.

The GaugeFactoryCL contract has no receive/fallback function, no deposit function, and no other mechanism to accept ERC20 tokens programmatically. While tokens can be sent directly via ERC20.transfer() to the factory address, this is an undocumented manual step. No deployment scripts in the repository fund the factory.

Note: safeApprove itself will NOT fail due to insufficient balance (approve doesn't check balance). The failure occurs when AlgebraEternalFarming attempts to pull the approved tokens via transferFrom. The hypothesis slightly mischaracterizes the exact failure point but the core defect is valid.

With 18 decimals, 1e10 base units = 0.00000001 tokens, which is economically trivial per gauge. However, the protocol-level impact is that no CL gauges can be created until someone manually sends tokens to the factory address.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the defect. GaugeFactoryCL.sol createEternalFarming() at lines 84-89 sets reward=1e10, calls safeApprove for that amount, and passes it to createEternalFarming on AlgebraEternalFarming. The factory contract has no token receipt mechanism. No deployment script funds it.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The function approves 1e10 of _rewardToken to _algebraEternalFarming and calls createEternalFarming with reward=1e10. The external AlgebraEternalFarming contract will attempt to transferFrom these tokens from GaugeFactoryCL. If the factory's balance is less than 1e10, the transferFrom reverts, blocking gauge creation. The contract has no built-in mechanism to receive tokens.

createGauge calls createEternalFarming as its first operation. If that fails due to insufficient token balance, the entire gauge creation reverts. This is the entry point called by GaugeManager._createGauge() for CL pools (gaugeType == 1).

calling_sequence: 1. User calls GaugeManager.createGauge(_pool, 1) for CL pool (gaugeType==1)
2. GaugeManager._createGauge() validates pool, tokens, deploys bribes
8. If GaugeFactoryCL balance < 1e10, transferFrom REVERTS, entire transaction fails
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The defect is in GaugeFactoryCL.createEternalFarming() (lines 78-96). The function assumes the factory holds reward tokens (reward=1e10 at line 84) and approves them for spending (line 88). The external AlgebraEternalFarming.createEternalFarming() call (line 93-94) will pull these tokens. Since GaugeFactoryCL has no deposit/receive mechanism and no deployment script funds it, the transferFrom will fail for any new deployment.

deployment_assumptions_checked: GaugeFactoryCL must be pre-funded with at least 1e10 base units of reward token (BLACK)
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Examined all provided deployment scripts. No script funds GaugeFactoryCL with BLACK tokens. Scripts cover: setReferralFee (setReferralFeeInGaugeFactory.js), setAlgebraPoolApi (setAlgebraPoolApiInGauge.js), setFarmingParam (setFarmingParamGaugeManager.js), and various other setup steps. None transfer tokens to GaugeFactoryCL. The factory initialization (GaugeFactoryCL.initialize) also does not accept or receive any tokens.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an external attack scenario but rather an operational defect. Any attempt to create a CL gauge (gaugeType == 1) will fail if GaugeFactoryCL has not been manually funded with reward tokens. The defect manifests immediately upon the first CL gauge creation attempt after deployment. Each subsequent gauge creation consumes an additional 1e10 base units, so even if initially funded, the factory must be periodically re-funded.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity for protocol operations. Without manual intervention (sending tokens directly to the factory address), no CL gauges can be created, blocking concentrated liquidity incentivization entirely. The amount needed per gauge (1e10 base units = ~0.00000001 tokens with 18 decimals) is economically trivial, but the operational gap is a real code defect. For a production deployment, this would cause immediate failure of CL gauge creation until discovered and manually resolved.
recommended_mitigations: Add a funding function to GaugeFactoryCL that allows authorized users to deposit reward tokens: function fundFactory(address _token, uint256 _amount) external onlyAllowed { IERC20(_token).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), _amount); }
Add a deployment script step that transfers sufficient reward tokens to GaugeFactoryCL after deployment
Consider having createEternalFarming pull tokens from the caller (GaugeManager) instead of requiring the factory to hold them, since GaugeManager already holds the base token
Add a check in createEternalFarming that verifies sufficient balance before proceeding: require(IERC20(_rewardToken).balanceOf(address(this)) >= reward, 'INSUFFICIENT_SEED')
Document the pre-funding requirement as a mandatory deployment step
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy GaugeFactoryCL and all dependencies per deployment scripts. 2. Do NOT send any BLACK tokens to GaugeFactoryCL. 3. Set up farming params via setFarmingParam. 4. Attempt to create a CL gauge by calling GaugeManager.createGauge(pool, 1). 5. Observe transaction revert during createEternalFarming when AlgebraEternalFarming attempts to pull 1e10 tokens from the unfunded factory.
uncertainties: The exact behavior of IAlgebraEternalFarmingCustom.createEternalFarming() is unknown as the contract is out of scope - it's possible (though unlikely) that it doesn't actually pull tokens during creation
It's possible the team has an undocumented manual step to fund the factory that isn't captured in the provided deployment scripts
The reward amount 1e10 with 18 decimals is ~0.00000001 tokens - economically trivial but still causes revert if absent
additional_testing_needed: Verify AlgebraEternalFarming.createEternalFarming() behavior: does it transferFrom the reward amount during creation?
Test CL gauge creation end-to-end with unfunded factory to confirm the revert
Verify whether safeApprove with amount=1e10 has any interaction issues (e.g., if previous approval was non-zero, OZ safeApprove requires it to be 0 first)
